articles.title: North Atlantic right whales now have a GoFundMe
articles.description:
A Cape Cod-based research group is turning to public donations to help fund efforts to protect one of the world’s most endangered whale species.

The post North Atlantic right whales now have a GoFundMe appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Cape Cod researchers launch GoFundMe to protect North Atlantic right whales.
Only about 340 North Atlantic right whales remain, highlighting their endangered status.
Local donations will directly support conservation efforts in the Boston area.

articles.title: ‘No Kings’ rallies draw crowds across US, in Europe. Springsteen headlines Minnesota demonstration.
articles.description:
“This reactionary nightmare, and these invasions of American cities, will not stand.”

The post ‘No Kings’ rallies draw crowds across US, in Europe. Springsteen headlines Minnesota demonstration. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Thousands attended 'No Kings' rallies across the US and Europe, protesting against perceived authoritarianism.
Bruce Springsteen headlined a major demonstration in Minnesota on March 29, 2026.
Boston residents may see increased activism and community engagement in response to these national protests.

articles.title: What to know about Trump’s order to pay TSA officers and its impact on airport security lines
articles.description:
Nearly 500 of the agency's nearly 50,000 officers have quit since the shutdown started, according to DHS.

The post What to know about Trump’s order to pay TSA officers and its impact on airport security lines appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Nearly 500 TSA officers have quit since the shutdown began, affecting airport security.
The TSA employs about 50,000 officers nationwide, highlighting significant staffing challenges.
Boston residents may experience longer airport security lines due to officer shortages.

articles.title: Iran-backed Houthis enter the monthlong war and could further threaten global shipping
articles.description:
Iran fired missiles toward Israel, while air defenses early Sunday intercepted missiles and drones across Gulf countries.

The post Iran-backed Houthis enter the monthlong war and could further threaten global shipping appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Iran-backed Houthis escalate conflict, threatening global shipping routes vital to Boston's economy.
Iran launched missiles towards Israel, heightening regional tensions and security concerns.
Boston's shipping industry may face disruptions, impacting local businesses and supply chains.
